The design's weakly supervised nature enhances adaptability and interpretability, making it a promising direction for future deepfake video detection systems.KEYWORDS: deepfake technologies, spatial-temporal model, attention mechanism, binary classification,

evidence.title: Targeted Sentiment Classification Based on Attentional Encoding and Graph Convolutional Networks
evidence.abstract: Targeted sentiment classification aims to predict the emotional trend of a specific goal. Currently, most methods (e.g., recurrent neural networks and convolutional neural networks combined with an attention mechanism) are not able to fully capture the semantic information of the context and they also lack a mechanism to explain the relevant syntactical constraints and long-range word dependencies. Therefore, syntactically irrelevant context words may mistakenly be recognized as clues to predict the target sentiment. To tackle these problems, this paper considers that the semantic information, syntactic information, and their interaction information are very crucial to targeted sentiment analysis, and propose an attentional-encoding-based graph convolutional network (AEGCN) model. Our proposed model is mainly composed of multi-head attention and an improved graph convolutional network built over the dependency tree of a sentence. Pre-trained BERT is applied to this task, and new state-of-art performance is achieved. Experiments on five datasets show the effectiveness of the model proposed in this paper compared with a series of the latest models.

evidence.title: Polarized-Self-Attention-enhanced TransUNet in medical image segmentation
evidence.venue: International Conference on Digital Image Processing
evidence.abstract: Transformer was initially used in the field of natural language processing, but has achieved remarkable success in the field of computer vision in recent years, especially the UNet-Transformer architecture, which takes into account the advantages of Convolutional Neural Network(CNN) in capturing detailed features and Transformer in capturing long-distance information. In response to the issue of detailed information loss during spatial and channel transformations in traditional encoderdecoder structures in the field of medical image segmentation, a new UNet-Transformer architecture—PSA-TransUNet has been proposed. PSA-TransUNet integrates channel and spatial dimension information by introducing the Polarized Self- Attention (PSA) module into both the encoder and decoder of the traditional TransUNet architecture, achieving synergistic enhancement of global semantics and local information without significantly increasing parameters. Additionally, it introduces AKConv during the skip connection process, which utilizes a parameterized dynamic kernel generation mechanism to more precisely fuse features from different levels. The PSA and AKConv modules enable the model to enhance local feature retention while maintaining global context modeling capabilities. Extensive comparative and ablation experiments were conducted on multiple medical image datasets, particularly achieving 0.930 Dice coefficient and 0.874 IoU metric on the GlaS dataset, validating the method’s effectiveness. The experimental results demonstrate that PSA-TransUNet performs well across various medical image segmentation tasks, especially in terms of Dice coefficient and IoU metric, proving the effectiveness and robustness of PSA-TransUNet.

evidence.title: Non-stationary fuzzy time series modeling and forecasting using deep learning with swarm optimization
evidence.venue: International Journal of Machine Learning and Cybernetics

evidence.title: The AI Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery
evidence.abstract: Introduces an automated scientific discovery pipeline that generates ideas, writes code, runs experiments, and drafts papers, while also exposing limits in evaluation and reliability.

evidence.title: The AI Scientist-v2: Workshop-Level Automated Scientific Discovery via Agentic Tree Search
evidence.abstract: Extends automated scientific discovery with stronger search and review-like iteration, motivating stricter gates for novelty, experiment quality, and paper readiness.

evidence.title: Agent Laboratory: Using LLM Agents as Research Assistants
evidence.abstract: Describes agentic research assistance across literature review, experimentation, and report writing, highlighting the need for human oversight and reproducibility controls.

evidence.title: MLE-bench: Evaluating Machine Learning Agents on Machine Learning Engineering
evidence.abstract: Benchmarks agents on machine learning engineering tasks and provides a reference point for evaluating experiment-running ability and reproducibility.

evidence.title: AIBuildAI-2: Scalable Agentic AI Research and Engineering
evidence.abstract: Frames AI systems that build and improve AI systems, including multi-agent research and engineering loops with traceability requirements.

evidence.title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
evidence.abstract: Uses verbal feedback and memory to help agents improve across trials, motivating explicit failure memory and revision loops.

evidence.title: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback
evidence.abstract: Studies iterative generation, feedback, and refinement, providing background for reviewer-style critique and revision policies.

evidence.title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
evidence.abstract: Demonstrates open-ended skill acquisition with memory and iterative exploration, relevant to long-running agent loops and durable lessons.

evidence.title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
evidence.abstract: Introduces agent evaluation tasks and motivates structured benchmarks for agent behavior rather than isolated text quality.

evidence.title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
evidence.abstract: Shows how agents can operate computer interfaces to solve software tasks, relevant to local experiment execution and reproducible command logs.
